channel.title: NPR Topics: National
channel.description: NPR coverage of national news, U.S. politics, elections, business, arts, culture, health and science, and technology. Subscribe to the NPR Nation RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: NASA's Artemis II crew readies for Monday's lunar flyby. Here's what you need to know
item.description: At its closest point, the crew of Artemis II will loop about 4,000 miles from the lunar surface late Monday. The astronauts will also venture farther into space than any previous human mission.
item.content:encoded:
At its closest point, the crew of Artemis II will loop about 4,000 miles from the lunar surface late Monday. The astronauts will also venture farther into space than any previous human mission.

(Image credit: NASA via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Brendan Byrne

item.title: UCLA storms past South Carolina to claim its 1st NCAA women's basketball title
item.description: UCLA secured the first NCAA women's basketball national championship in school history — a goal that was set after losing in the first Final Four last season.
item.content:encoded:
UCLA secured the first NCAA women's basketball national championship in school history — a goal that was set after losing in the first Final Four last season.

(Image credit: Rick Scuteri)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: More teens are getting hooked on gambling. Parents say it often goes undetected
item.description: The explosion of online gambling and sports betting, as well as the advertising behind it, is attracting a growing number of young people, most of them boys.
item.content:encoded: The explosion of online gambling and sports betting, as well as the advertising behind it, is attracting a growing number of young people, most of them boys.
item.dc:creator: Sequoia Carrillo

item.title: Judge halts Trump effort requiring colleges to show they don't consider race in admissions
item.description: A federal judge on Saturday said the Trump Administration the demand to collect data from universities was rolled out in a "rushed and chaotic" manner.
item.content:encoded:
A federal judge on Saturday said the Trump Administration the demand to collect data from universities was rolled out in a "rushed and chaotic" manner.

(Image credit: Alex Brandon)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: After the Minnesota surge, ICE is moving to a quieter enforcement approach
item.description: ICE seems to be changing from aggressive immigration enforcement on city streets to an apparent return to operations that rely heavily on local law enforcement. But even in Florida, where sheriffs are required to cooperate with ICE, some conservative sheriffs have concerns about pursuing immigrants with no criminal records.
item.content:encoded:
ICE seems to be changing from aggressive immigration enforcement on city streets to an apparent return to operations that rely heavily on local law enforcement. But even in Florida, where sheriffs are required to cooperate with ICE, some conservative sheriffs have concerns about pursuing immigrants with no criminal records.

(Image credit: Joe Raedle)
item.dc:creator: Meg Anderson

item.title: The story of a 'Dreamer' who was wrongfully deported and has now returned to the U.S.
item.description: Maria de Jesús Estrada Juárez returned to the U.S. this week after being wrongfully deported. NPR's Scott Simon speaks with Estrada Juárez and her daughter, Damaris Bello, about their experience.
item.content:encoded: Maria de Jesús Estrada Juárez returned to the U.S. this week after being wrongfully deported. NPR's Scott Simon speaks with Estrada Juárez and her daughter, Damaris Bello, about their experience.
item.dc:creator: Scott Simon

item.title: How a free pop-up health clinic in Texas is reaching a community of fishermen
item.description: Amid a rise in healthcare costs and declining federal support for commercial fishermen, a free pop-up health clinic in Galveston, Texas is reaching a community of Vietnamese fishermen.
item.content:encoded: Amid a rise in healthcare costs and declining federal support for commercial fishermen, a free pop-up health clinic in Galveston, Texas is reaching a community of Vietnamese fishermen.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Myong

item.title: Iran war enters its 6th week as military searches for downed jet crew member
item.description: The war in Iran enters its 6th week as the search continues for the missing U.S. service member who bailed out of a fighter jet shot down over Iran on Friday.
item.content:encoded:
The war in Iran enters its 6th week as the search continues for the missing U.S. service member who bailed out of a fighter jet shot down over Iran on Friday.

(Image credit: Majid Saeedi)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: Buttercream wool and jelly bean eyes: The art of the Easter lamb cake
item.description: The cakes – usually baked in the shape of a lamb using a special pan – have a long history in Central Europe, from the German osterlamm, to the Polish baranek wielkanocny, to the Alsatian lammele.
item.content:encoded:
The cakes – usually baked in the shape of a lamb using a special pan – have a long history in Central Europe, from the German osterlamm, to the Polish baranek wielkanocny, to the Alsatian lammele.

(Image credit: Charra Jarosz)
item.dc:creator: Deena Prichep

item.title: When legal sports betting surges, so do Americans' financial problems
item.description: As online betting has grown in popularity, a new report from the New York Federal Reserve builds on the troubling link between legal sports wagering and financial health.
item.content:encoded:
As online betting has grown in popularity, a new report from the New York Federal Reserve builds on the troubling link between legal sports wagering and financial health.

(Image credit: Charlie Riedel)
item.dc:creator: Alana Wise
